blockedWork.title: Make tracking ingestion Kafka consumer batch-ready
blockedWork.scope: Add a tracking-service-specific Spring Kafka batch listener factory, change tracking.ingested consumption to receive EventEnvelope batches directly, preserve fail-closed tenant/workspace/idempotency behavior, prevent replay after non-idempotent ClickHouse success, and add focused listener/factory tests.
blockedWork.nonGoals: Do not claim production throughput, alter Kafka trusted packages, change ClickHouse schema, or weaken signed tracking/outbox/idempotency behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The tracking.ingested listener directly accepts List<EventEnvelope<String>> and references a named tracking batch listener factory.
The tracking batch factory sets batchListener=true, AckMode.BATCH, configurable concurrency, and bounded max.poll.records without widening trusted packages.
Envelope eventId, tenantId, workspaceId, payload, and eventType validation happens before idempotency claims or ClickHouse side effects.
Duplicate/idempotency-rejected events are skipped without ClickHouse writes, aggregation, processed marks, or claim releases.
A valid multi-record batch is written to ClickHouse in one writeBatch call and processed claims are marked only after aggregation succeeds.
ClickHouse write failure releases claimed events for Kafka retry, while aggregation/mark failure after ClickHouse success does not release claims for duplicate raw inserts.
Focused tests cover listener annotation/factory wiring, batch factory settings, valid batch write behavior, duplicate filtering, mismatch fail-closed behavior, and retry release semantics.
blockers: Docker/PostgreSQL/Flyway proof, live ClickHouse duplicate/reconciliation evidence, raw-event export/BI semantics, and target runtime metrics are not present in repository.
blockedWork.nextAction: Collect Docker/PostgreSQL and live ClickHouse evidence for idempotency, ambiguous/partial batch writes, physical raw-event duplicate behavior, and downstream reconciliation before marking DONE or making BI/throughput claims.
blockedWork.outcome: Local tracking ingestion batch-consumer hardening is implemented and validated: tracking now uses a dedicated long-horizon batch Kafka error handler, raw-write/finalization ledger phases, duplicate batch filtering, Docker-independent migration checks, and ClickHouse local/runtime schema repair for writer lineage columns. It is not DONE because Docker/Testcontainers Postgres proof and live ClickHouse raw_events dedupe/reconciliation evidence are unavailable.

PASS: git diff --check -- services/tracking-service shared/legent-kafka infrastructure/docker/local .codex (CRLF warnings only)
